name: Atomic Skill Design
description: Principles for creating stateless, single-purpose, and verifiable agent skills.

single_responsibility.name: Single Responsibility
single_responsibility.description: Principles for create single-purpose and verifiable agent skills.
single_responsibility.context: Defining the scope of a new skill.
single_responsibility.implementation: Each skill MUST perform exactly one logical operation (e.g., 'Git Commit', 'RAG Search'). If it branches into unrelated logic, split it.

verifiable_execution.name: Verifiable Execution
verifiable_execution.description: Every SKILL.md MUST include a 'Verification' section with a concrete command to test the skill's output.
verifiable_execution.implementation: Every SKILL.md MUST include a 'Verification' section with a concrete command to test the skill's output.

best_practices: Prefer statelessness over complex internal caching within the skill.
Use standard MCP tool names for interoperability.
Document all environment variable dependencies clearly.
anti_patterns: God-Skills that attempt to handle entire workflows alone.
Skills that depend on specific user state without clear error messages.
title: Atomic-skill-patterns Knowledge

axiomAlignment.A1_verifiability: Patterns are verified through automated testing.
axiomAlignment.A2_user_primacy: The user maintains control over all generated output.
axiomAlignment.A3_transparency: All automated actions are logged and verifiable.
axiomAlignment.A4_non_harm: Strict safety checks prevent destructive operations.
axiomAlignment.A5_consistency: Uniform patterns ensure predictable system behavior.
